items.q: What should an analytical essay on a set text mainly do?
items.correct: Analyse how themes and techniques create meaning
items.misconception: Retell the whole plot in order
items.why: Students retell the story instead of analysing it; examiners reward analysis supported by quotation.

items.q: Why include short quotations ('citations') in an essay?
items.correct: To provide textual evidence for your points
items.misconception: To make the essay longer for no reason
items.why: Students either avoid quotations or quote at length; brief, well-chosen citations support analytical points.

items.q: 'La mise en scène' is a concept used to analyse what?
items.why: Students apply literary-only terms to film; mise en scène is specific to how a director stages a scene.

items.q: In a literary essay, why do you include 'des citations'?
items.correct: To provide textual evidence supporting your analysis
items.misconception: To make the essay longer with no purpose
items.why: It's To provide textual evidence supporting your analysis. “To make the essay longer with no purpose” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: 'La mise en scène' is a key concept when analysing which kind of set work?
items.why: Correct answer: A film. “A poem only” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A strong analytical essay on a set text should mainly:
items.correct: Analyse how themes and techniques create meaning
items.misconception: Simply retell the plot from start to finish
items.random_wrong: List all the characters' names only
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Analyse how themes and techniques create meaning. “Simply retell the plot from start to finish” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: The main character. “The setting” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
